expectations.text: Review includes a complexity/split advisory (3 layers: Core, API, Frontend across 27+ files)
expectations.evidence: The review header notes '29 total' files and 'Skipped: 2 noise files' but does not include any complexity advisory or suggestion to split the PR. The review covers Core, API, and Frontend findings but does not flag the multi-layer nature as a complexity concern.

expectations.text: Flags new interface methods without default implementations as breaking changes (Pattern 3)
expectations.evidence: Two Critical findings explicitly flag this: IUserService.UpdateCurrentUserAsync and IUserPresentationFactory.CreateUpdateCurrentUserModelAsync are both called out as lacking default implementations, with Pattern 3 referenced and code examples for fixes including TODO comments for V19 removal.

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues unless they affect performance or rendering
expectations.evidence: All findings are substantive: breaking interface changes, missing ISO code validation, modal error handling, missing tests, AbortController misuse, scope/transaction issue, naming inconsistency (minor but functional). No formatting/whitespace/cosmetic issues flagged.

expectations.text: All findings reference code that was changed in the diff, not pre-existing issues
expectations.evidence: All findings reference new code added in the PR: IUserService.cs new method, IUserPresentationFactory.cs new method, UserService.cs new UpdateCurrentUserAsync, new modal element, new controllers, new repository methods. No pre-existing issues flagged.

expectations.text: Review assesses the new feature's architecture, patterns, or integration approach -- not just absence of bugs
expectations.evidence: The review assesses architectural patterns: the breaking interface change pattern (Pattern 3), the missing ICoreScope for transaction safety in ClearAvatarAsync, and the modal/save error handling architecture. The suggestions about AbortController lifecycle and naming consistency also show architectural thinking.
